notes: Server-independent file-level storage over Ethernet (NFS/SMB). NAS devices began gaining popularity mid-1990s. Now a mainstream enterprise and cloud storage paradigm.
High-performance block-level storage network using Fibre Channel. Enterprise SAN market exploded in the late 1990s/2000s.
Traditional server-centric storage. The 'decades-old architecture' Aberdeen targets as becoming inadequate.
Primary SAN interconnect protocol in late 1990s. Still used in high-performance enterprise environments.
Core UNIX NAS protocol. Sun Microsystems introduced NFS in 1984; became standard by the mid-1990s.
